entities.description: The U-M manually keyed electronic text represented by stable item identifier CME00133 and preserved in the repository artifact.
entities.label: University of Michigan Corpus of Middle English Prose and Verse encoding CME00133
notes: The encoding selects the 1890 title page, pp. 3-272, the p. 273 section heading, and the parallel text beginning p. 275. Kölbing's introduction, blank pp. 2 and 274, Lovelich appendix, notes, register, and glossary are outside the continuous encoded text.
Page and folio milestones preserve Kölbing's printed alignment and historical manuscript references.

rights.basis: The U-M collection describes the keyed texts as derived from public-domain editions and marks the collection No Copyright - United States.

bibliographic.citation: Eugen Kölbing, ed., Arthour and Merlin, nach der Auchinleck-Hs. Nebst zwei Beilagen, Altenglische Bibliothek 4 (Leipzig: O. R. Reisland, 1890).
bibliographic.title: Arthour and Merlin, nach der Auchinleck-Hs. Nebst zwei Beilagen

entities.description: A scholarly edition of the Auchinleck version followed by a parallel younger-version edition using L/P/H and D, with introduction, Lovelich appendix, textual notes, register, and glossary.
entities.label: Eugen Kölbing's 1890 Arthour and Merlin edition
notes: Kölbing says the Auchinleck text closely follows the manuscript and that he re-collated proofs against it in situ.
L is his base for the younger class-a stream; D is printed separately; substantive P/H readings appear in notes and P supplies missing L passages through Hales/Furnivall.
The Lovelich specimen from CCCC MS 80 and Mary Bateson's collaboration are outside CME00133's encoded literary streams.
The Auchinleck text is printed on pp. 1-272; the younger parallel version on pp. 273-370; Lovelich on pp. 371-408; annotations and reference matter on pp. 409-503.

rights.basis: The edition was published in 1890 and Kölbing died in 1899. This assessment concerns the original 1890 edition, not a modern wrapper, scan, OCR, or repository encoding.
rights.component: 1890 editorial text, notes, typography, and organization
rights.jurisdiction: United States and jurisdictions applying life plus 70 years

access.accessMethod: Open the NLS manuscript catalogue and dedicated Auchinleck headnote.
access.entityLabel: National Library of Scotland, Advocates MS 19.2.1 (Auchinleck Manuscript)
access.format: HTML catalogue and scholarly headnote

entities.description: A major Middle English literary codex made probably in London in 1331-1340; Scribe 1 copied Arthour and Merlin at current ff. 201rb-256vb.

holding.notes: The work occupies current ff. 201rb-256vb; Kölbing's four-column notation abbreviates this as 201b-256b.

entities.label: National Library of Scotland, Advocates MS 19.2.1 (Auchinleck Manuscript)
notes: The extant/encoded text has 9,763 actual lines; Kölbing's editorial positions reach 9,938 and DIMEV preserves an additional 9,772-line source count.
The last 6,774 lines are unique to this witness according to the NLS headnote.
The opening miniature was cut out, damaging verses 34-44.
A lost leaf after f. 254 removes about 172 verses after the position numbered 9,446.

rights.basis: The manuscript dates to 1331-1340. This assessment concerns medieval wording only, not modern catalogue descriptions, photography, or transcriptions.
rights.component: Medieval manuscript wording of Arthour and Merlin

dateStatements.label: Production
dateStatements.notes: The NLS headnote supplies the c.1450 date; Lincoln's Inn's broad viewer description instead calls the hand fourteenth-century.

entities.description: A Middle English romance codex whose Arthour and Merlin copy occupies current ff. 11r-27v and is the principal base of Kölbing's younger class-a stream.

holding.notes: Current work locus ff. 11r-27v. Kölbing's legacy sequence f. 13, ff. 2-3, ff. 14-27v maps respectively to current f. 11, ff. 12-13, and ff. 14-27v.

entities.label: Lincoln's Inn, Hale MS 150
notes: Kölbing says L is missing a leaf between his old ff. 3 and 14 and that its own text reaches his verse 1,980; P supplies the lacuna and terminal continuation.
The Shropshire localization is linguistic/textual; it is not treated as an independently proven place of codex manufacture.
The complete public object exposes 264 images including binding and end matter.
The work begins on public image 29 and ends with Explicit Merlin on image 62.

rights.basis: The manuscript is medieval. This assessment concerns the wording only and does not extend to Lincoln's Inn photographs, website text, or downloadable files.
rights.component: Medieval manuscript wording of Arthour and Merlin

rights.basis: Access or delivery does not itself establish publication or redistribution rights. Exact terms depend on the service, quote, order conditions, delivered-file notice, and any written permission.

access.accessMethod: Register for a Bodleian Reader card and request the codex under current Special Collections rules.
access.format: Original parchment codex

entities.description: A fifteenth-century English parchment codex containing an Arthour and Merlin version imperfect at both ends and with a gap after f. 23.

holding.notes: The current catalogue infers the item at ff. 1r-37v; Kölbing and DIMEV identify their text as ff. 1-36v. The terminal discrepancy has not been resolved by a complete facsimile.

entities.label: Oxford, Bodleian Library, MS Douce 236
notes: Kölbing prints this as the separate D-family stream on pp. 275-355.
Only three selected openings are public; they overlap the work but do not establish the whole item boundary.
Written in Anglicana; the current catalogue records about 1,300 lines and about 26 lost at the opening.
A gap follows f. 23.

rights.basis: The manuscript dates to the fifteenth century. This assessment concerns medieval wording only, not modern catalogue encoding, photography, or supplied scans.
rights.component: Medieval manuscript wording of Arthour and Merlin

rights.basis: Access or delivery does not itself establish publication or redistribution rights. Exact terms depend on the service, quote, order conditions, delivered-file notice, and any written permission.

bibliographic.citation: John W. Hales and Frederick J. Furnivall, eds., Bishop Percy's Folio Manuscript: Ballads and Romances, vol. 1 (London: N. Trübner, 1867-68).
bibliographic.title: Bishop Percy's Folio Manuscript: Ballads and Romances

entities.description: The printed transcript of the Percy Folio's ballads and romances through which Kölbing obtained P-supplied Arthour and Merlin passages.
entities.label: Hales and Furnivall's Bishop Percy's Folio Manuscript, volume 1
notes: The full Arthur/Merlin component occupies pp. 401-496; its introduction is pp. 401-416, the P transcript begins on p. 417, and Kölbing specifically cites pp. 422-496 for the passages corresponding to his P supply.
Kölbing says he silently corrected small inaccuracies in the printed transcript, but his wording does not securely establish direct codex inspection.

rights.basis: The volume was published in 1867-68 and both editors died more than seventy years ago. This assessment excludes the modern scan and OCR layers.
rights.component: 1867-68 printed transcript, editorial text, and organization
rights.jurisdiction: United States and jurisdictions applying life plus 70 years

next_steps: Search Kölbing's correspondence, working papers, and any fuller publication history for an explicit Percy Folio inspection statement.
Retain the current Kölbing -> Hales/Furnivall -> P chain unless direct codex use is affirmatively documented.
notes: Kölbing's statement that he corrected small inaccuracies in the printed edition does not by itself prove direct manuscript consultation.
openQuestions.question: Did Kölbing directly inspect BL Add MS 27879 when correcting small errors in Hales/Furnivall, or did he work only from the printed transcript and other comparison evidence?

next_steps: Obtain images of current ff. 2r-2v and compare physical lines with Turnbull's transcript and DIMEV's counting convention.
openQuestions.question: Why does DIMEV count the Harley 6223 excerpt as 61 lines while the British Library catalogue and Kölbing describe 62?

next_steps: Inspect a complete current folio sequence or request reference scans of ff. 36r-37v.
Compare historical and current foliation marks before asserting an exact crosswalk.
notes: The three public openings do not include the terminal leaves and cannot resolve the discrepancy.
openQuestions.question: How exactly does Kölbing/DIMEV's ff. 1-36v text boundary map to the current Bodleian catalogue's inferred ff. 1r-37v item boundary?

next_steps: Ask Bodleian whether an existing internal surrogate can be supplied before commissioning new photography.
If none exists, request a quote for reference scans of the full codex or at minimum ff. 1r-37v.
openQuestions.question: Is a complete public or privately requestable existing digital facsimile of MS Douce 236 available beyond the three selected Digital Bodleian openings?

next_steps: Ask BL Digitisation Services whether legacy photography exists and can be supplied.
If necessary, request manuscript pp. 145-178 first and a separate whole-object quotation.
openQuestions.question: Is a complete public or privately requestable existing digital facsimile of BL Add MS 27879 available?

next_steps: Ask BL Digitisation Services whether legacy photography exists and can be supplied.
If necessary, request current ff. 2r-2v first and a separate whole-object quotation.
openQuestions.question: Is a complete public or privately requestable existing digital facsimile of BL Harley MS 6223 available?

next_steps: Consult the full Lincoln's Inn manuscript catalogue and current palaeographical bibliography.
Continue to qualify the c.1450 date rather than flattening the conflict.
openQuestions.question: How should the NLS headnote's c.1450 date for Hale 150 be reconciled with Lincoln's Inn's broad description of a fourteenth-century hand?

next_steps: Seek a repository-level clarification or data-specific rights statement.
Keep the repository-artifact rights layer unknown while retaining separate public-domain and provider-rights assessments for source layers.
openQuestions.question: Does the CME repository's Unlicense dedication expressly cover the corpus XML data and editorial markup, or only software?

reviewNotes: The primary chain is repository artifact -> U-M encoding -> Kölbing 1890 edition -> manuscripts and named printed intermediaries.
The XML's first global DIV1 is the editorial title page. The Auchinleck text is DIV1[2], and the younger supplement is DIV1[3] with L/P and D as DIV2[1] and DIV2[2].

Kölbing's L stream is composite: Hale 150 supplies the surviving base through his verse 1,980, while P supplies a missing leaf and the terminal 612-line continuation through the Hales/Furnivall transcript. P and H also contribute selected apparatus readings.
The current Hale 150 work locus is ff. 11r-27v. Kölbing's legacy sequence f. 13, ff. 2-3, ff. 14-27v maps to current f. 11, ff. 12-13, ff. 14-27v and is preserved in both witness and relation scopes.

For Douce 236, Kölbing and DIMEV give ff. 1-36v, whereas the current Bodleian catalogue infers the item across ff. 1r-37v. The relation uses the historical source locator and qualifies the current boundary rather than asserting an exact one-to-one crosswalk.
The three Digital Bodleian surfaces are a partial selection, not a complete witness facsimile. Their exact labels are fol. 013v-14r, fol. 021v-022r, and fol. 022v-023r.
Turnbull's 1838 Auchinleck edition is a consulted predecessor and comparison source, not the base of Kölbing's main text; its distinct Harley-fragment role remains explicit.

The Internet Archive Kölbing PDF scans a 1968 Rodopi facsimile reissue containing the 1890 edition. The reissue/scan is a separate facsimile entity so its modern wrapper and uncertain scan rights are not conflated with the public-domain 1890 editorial text.

lineage.summary: The repository artifact is a pinned copy of University of Michigan CME00133, which encodes selected pages of Eugen Kölbing's 1890 Arthour and Merlin. The encoding reproduces Kölbing's title page, the Auchinleck text on printed pp. 3-272, and the parallel younger-version streams on pp. 275-370 (L/P composite) and pp. 275-355 (D). Kölbing transcribed the older version directly from NLS Adv.MS.19.2.1, used Lincoln's Inn Hale 150 as the principal younger L base, and transcribed Bodleian MS Douce 236 as a separate D-family stream. For text supplied from the Percy Folio he explicitly used Hales and Furnivall's 1867-68 printed transcript, and his Harley 6223 readings pass through Turnbull's 1838 edition; neither printed intermediary is collapsed into a direct manuscript edge. Complete public copies of Kölbing, Hales/Furnivall, and Turnbull are cached, as are complete provider-exposed facsimiles of the Auchinleck Manuscript and Hale 150. Digital Bodleian publishes only three selected Douce 236 openings, all overlapping the work but constituting partial manuscript coverage. No complete public facsimile was found for Douce 236, Add MS 27879, or Harley MS 6223, so current catalog, physical-access, and reproduction-request routes are recorded.

catalogSummary.author: Anonymous
catalogSummary.date: Composed about 1250-1300; first attested in the Auchinleck Manuscript, 1331-1340
catalogSummary.region: Kent (composition); represented witnesses have London, Shropshire, Dorset, and Lancashire associations
catalogSummary.title: Of Arthour and of Merlin

catalogingSubject.label: Of Arthour and of Merlin
catalogingSubject.scope_note: One anonymous Middle English Arthurian romance represented in CME00133 by an Auchinleck version and two parallel streams of a younger couplet recension. Kölbing's L/P composite stream and D stream are alternate versions, not additional intrinsic sections to be summed with the Auchinleck text.

contentParts.label: Of Arthour and of Merlin

locators.value: DIV1[2-3] (literary streams); DIV1[1] is editorial title-page paratext

notes: The parent count is one abstract work. Parallel versions are child streams with item_count 0 and are not summed as separate works.
The encoded German title page and editorial paratext are representation-level matter, not separate literary items.
contentParts.summary: The abstract romance represented by one older Auchinleck version and two parallel younger-version streams.

contentParts.label: Auchinleck version
notes: The 9,763 value is the actual encoded and extant verse-line count. Kölbing's editorial numbering reaches 9,938 because damaged and lost passages retain inferred positions; DIMEV also preserves a 9,772-line source note.
Kölbing's old four-column notation 201b-256b corresponds to the current locator ff. 201rb-256vb.
contentParts.summary: The older version copied by Auchinleck Scribe 1, printed by Kölbing from the manuscript and encoded from printed pp. 3-272.

contentParts.label: Younger L/P composite version

locators.value: ff. 11r-27v (legacy Kölbing loci: f. 13, ff. 2-3, ff. 14-27v)

notes: Kölbing says the L manuscript itself reaches his verse 1,980. P supplies the missing leaf and a terminal continuation of 612 lines; the P passages reach Kölbing through Hales and Furnivall's printed transcript.
DIMEV calls L a 2,490-line text because Kölbing's numbered series ends at 2,490. The XML contains two additional final prayer lines and therefore has 2,492 L elements.
H is a short collated fragment, not a continuous base text for this stream.
contentParts.summary: Kölbing's class-a stream: Lincoln's Inn Hale 150 (L) is the base through its surviving text, while the Percy Folio (P) supplies L's missing leaf and the terminal continuation; P and Harley 6223 (H) also contribute selected apparatus readings.

contentParts.label: Younger D version

locators.value: Bodleian MS Douce 236, ff. 1r-37v; Kölbing/DIMEV text locator ff. 1-36v

notes: DIMEV reports 1,278 lines, matching the numbered series. The XML has two fragmentary pre-numbering L elements and therefore 1,280 encoded verse lines.
The current Bodleian catalogue infers the work across ff. 1r-37v and records a gap after f. 23; the older Kölbing/DIMEV locator ends at f. 36v.
contentParts.summary: The separate younger-version family represented by Bodleian MS Douce 236 (D), defective at both beginning and end and with an internal leaf loss.

assertion.notes: The Database of Middle English Romance gives the interval 1250-1300; this is a scholarly dating, not a dated authorial witness.

extent.description: One abstract verse romance in three parallel encoded streams: 9,763 lines in the Auchinleck version, 2,492 XML L elements in the younger L/P composite, and 1,280 XML L elements in the younger D version. The parent verse-line count is intentionally null because parallel versions must not be summed as the extent of one work.

formStatements.description: Narrative verse in short rhyming couplets

notes: The XML includes Kölbing's German title page, section heading, and editorial notes. German is documented at the edition and paratext layers rather than exposed as a language facet of the Middle English work.
The younger L/P and D streams begin on the same printed page and run in parallel; repeated page references in those streams are not sequential pagination errors.
Lovelich's separate Merlin text from Cambridge, Corpus Christi College MS 80 follows Kölbing's younger-version edition but is outside CME00133 and is not part of this work record.
Bodleian MS Douce 124 is a circa-1800 transcript collateral to the Auchinleck transmission history, not an ancestor of Kölbing's CME text.

next_steps: Compare additional modern scholarship on recension-level work boundaries.
Retain the current one-work model unless a corpus-wide policy requires recension-level splitting.
notes: DIMEV indexes the incipits separately but cross-references them; the NLS headnote and Kölbing explicitly frame the texts as versions of the same romance.
openQuestions.question: Should the younger couplet recension eventually receive a separate abstract-work record rather than remain parallel version streams under the same romance?

next_steps: Build a milestone-to-L-element concordance across each stream.
Compare DIMEV's source notes with Macrae-Gibson's numbering conventions.
notes: The current manifest reports actual XML L counts and preserves every conflicting editorial/catalog count rather than forcing equivalence.
openQuestions.question: Can the 9,763, 9,772, and 9,938 main-version counts and the 2,490/2,492 and 1,278/1,280 younger-version counts be fully reconciled in a future machine-readable line concordance?

scope.description: Localization associated with the Percy Folio copy
Editor of all three text streams as printed in 1890 and represented electronically
Co-editor of the Percy Folio transcript used by Kölbing for passages supplied from P
Co-editor of the Percy Folio transcript used by Kölbing for passages supplied from P
Editor of the printed Harley 6223 fragment through which Kölbing reports H readings

subjects.label: Kingship, succession, conquest, and political legitimacy

summaries.value: A long Middle English couplet romance recounting the political and martial history that leads from Vortigern and Uther Pendragon to Arthur's rise, with Merlin's birth, prophecies, supernatural knowledge, disguises, and counsel central to the narrative.

CME00133 encodes Kölbing's Auchinleck text and both columns of his younger-version edition. The L/P composite and D texts are parallel recensional streams; P and H also enter through printed intermediary transcriptions and apparatus readings.

assertion.notes: A normalized cataloging title based on the Auchinleck rubric and Kölbing's heading; it is not asserted as authorial.

scope.description: Kölbing's edition title and common scholarly title
Editorial section heading for the two younger-version streams

titles.value: Jesu Crist, heuen king, / Al ous graunt gode ending

scope.description: DIMEV normalized incipit for the younger L/P/H family; the D witness is defective at the beginning

titles.value: He that made with His hand
